strengths: Three distinct objects are clearly identified and linked, showing basic real-world contexts.
Some attempt at justification through cultural significance (e.g., spiritual use of the dream catcher).
Occasional citation of sources demonstrates minimal engagement with evidence.
weaknesses: Real-world contexts and provenance details are vague or unsupported by precise sourcing.
Links to the IA prompt remain descriptive and repetitive, lacking critical comparison or depth.
Analysis is surface-level with limited integration of key TOK concepts (shared vs personal knowledge).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work aligns with the 3â4 band: the student presents three objects with minimal real-world grounding and offers descriptive, repetitive links to the prompt âDoes some knowledge belong only to particular communities of knowers?â While there is some justification for each object and occasional citations, the explanations lack precision, sustained relevance and depth of analysis. Given the total mark of 3, this criterion score reflects the clear but unconvincing and underdeveloped nature of the exhibitionâs explanations and evidence support.

annotations.comment: The title repeats the IA prompt but lacks a focused thesis linking the chosen objects explicitly to the questionâs core knowledge issue.
The introduction defines personal knowledge metaphorically but does not integrate key TOK terminology (e.g. shared vs. personal knowledge), weakening its theoretical foundation.
How does this generic caption connect the Dream Catcher to the IA prompt? Clarify why this specific object was chosen rather than any dream catcher.
The image is uncited and lacks contextual details (provenance, date of photograph), which undermines its real-world grounding.
The origin date âaround 1929â is asserted without a precise source or rationale. Provide evidence or reference to substantiate the claimed creation period.
The descriptive legend of Asibikaashi lacks explicit linkage to the knowledge question. Connect how this myth reinforces or challenges the idea of exclusive community knowledge.
Good attempt to illustrate cultural appropriation, but the argument would benefit from TOK concepts (e.g. language barriers vs. communal memory) to deepen analysis.
Several online list items lack uniform title capitalization and italics for book titles; apply consistent stylistic rules.
This online tour site citation lacks an access date; add 'Accessed DD Mon YYYY' for completeness.
The final Studenti.it entry is truncated and missing publication details; complete the citation with author and date.
URL in this entry splits mid-sentence and is unclear; ensure each web citation is a single, complete URL.
The TrevisoEmozioni citation omits publisher and access date; include all mandatory elements for web sources.
This entry lacks URL and publisher information; add the full link and date accessed.
The standalone domain line provides no context; integrate it with the preceding or succeeding citation for clarity.
Good inclusion of access date, but place it immediately after the URL in parentheses to maintain consistency.
The book image lacks edition details or publication specifics, weakening the real-world context.
Inconsistent dates (1550 vs. 1558) appear without explanation. Clarify the publication timeline to strengthen historical context.
The restriction of knowers to Italian speakers is identified but lacks counter-examples (e.g. non-Italian experts). This would deepen the evaluation.
The personal photo of the Neapolitan horn is uncited and lacks provenance details (e.g. acquisition date, maker), reducing context precision.
The Neolithic origin date (3500 B.C.) is stated without referencing archaeological evidence or scholarly sources.
The concluding paragraph reasserts prior claims but adds no new analytical insight; consider synthesizing across objects for a stronger conclusion.
The word count is clearly indicated but lacks relevance to assessment; focus the reader on content rather than administrative details.
Works cited heading correctly introduced the list but would benefit from specifying citation style (e.g. MLA or APA).
This citation lacks consistent formatting and omitted access dates in parentheses; follow a single style guide for all entries.
The Google Books entry is overly long and missing publisher location; trim URLs and include essential bibliographic details only.
The HARPO citation contains a malformed math tag and incomplete URL; correct the formatting and ensure the full link is presented.
Reliance on Wikipedia is risky; seek primary or peer-reviewed sources to support key historical claims.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
